user.title: Connect Fraimic E-Ink Canvas
user.description: Use the frame's IP address, not the fraimic.local hostname -- recommended, since fraimic.local can point to the wrong frame if you ever have more than one on your network (see Fraimic's own guide, issue #1). Find the IP on the frame's local web interface or your router's DHCP client list.

reconfigure.title: Change Fraimic address
reconfigure.description: Update your frame's address. Use its IP address rather than fraimic.local -- see the note on the initial setup step for why.

error.cannot_connect: Couldn't connect. Check that the frame is awake and connected.

abort.wrong_device: This address belongs to a different physical Fraimic frame than the one you're reconfiguring.

invalid_image_size.message: File is not the correct size (expected exactly 960,000 bytes).

file_too_large.message: File is larger than the frame's 1 MB limit.

unsupported_content_type.message: The frame only accepts .bin files, not JPEG/PNG.

buffer_not_ready.message: Frame is still busy (e.g. mid-refresh). Try again shortly.

charging_cable_connected.message: Can't sleep: a charging cable is plugged into the frame.

path_not_allowed.message: Path '{path}' is not in Home Assistant's allowlist_external_dirs.

already_busy.message: Still processing the previous image, wait a moment and try again.

send_image.name: Send image
send_image.description: Converts a local image file to the Spectra 6 .bin format and sends it to the Fraimic frame. Handy from automations/scripts.

path.name: Path
path.description: Full path to the image file (jpg/png/etc.), e.g. /config/www/fraimic/photo.jpg. Must be within an allowed directory (allowlist_external_dirs or /config/www).

fit.name: Fit
fit.description: How to fit the image into the 1200x1600 frame: fit (show the whole image, pad with black -- CSS object-fit: contain) or fill (fill the frame, cropping overflow -- CSS object-fit: cover).

dither.name: Dithering
dither.description: Dithering algorithm for reducing the image to 6 colors, via the epaper-dithering library (falls back to floyd_steinberg for ordered/burkes/stucki/sierra/sierra_lite/jarvis_judice_ninke if that library isn't available): none (hard edges, banding), floyd_steinberg (most common, good default), atkinson (matches the original Fraimic conversion tool), ordered (fast, visible pattern), burkes, stucki (wide error distribution), sierra, sierra_lite (fast, simple), jarvis_judice_ninke (smooth gradients, slowest).

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: Converts the image and updates the Display entity's picture like normal, but skips actually uploading it to the frame. Handy for previewing fit/dither/device_orientation results without waiting through the frame's 20-30s refresh cycle each time.

init.title: Fraimic display defaults
init.description: Used when tapping an image in the media browser, which has no way to pass extra parameters per tap. The fraimic.send_image service can always override fit/dither per call (device orientation is always used from here, since it's a physical fact about the frame, not something that varies per image).
